sections.heading: Welcome to NEXUS
sections.content: NEXUS (Ghost Nexus OS) is a production-grade AI-powered stereo vision and laser targeting system. It fuses dual USB cameras, YOLOv11 pose estimation, Kalman filtering, stereo triangulation, and a hardware-controlled pan/tilt laser module into a single real-time tracking platform. Designed for precision, NEXUS can lock, track, and illuminate a moving person from a distance with millimetric servo accuracy.

sections.heading: System Overview
sections.content: NEXUS is built on five core pillars that work together in a real-time pipeline:
list: AI Vision Engine (VisionEngine — QThread) — Runs YOLOv11 pose estimation at 30+ FPS, handles stereo frame capture, target locking, and telemetry emission.
Kalman Smoother (GhostPredictor) — Smooths raw AI detections using a Kalman filter with configurable intensity. Returns corrected current positions, never predicted future ones.
Stereo Triangulation — Uses dual-camera epipolar geometry to compute 3D depth of the target using the formula: depth = (focal_px × baseline_cm) / disparity.
Homography Transform (CalibrationMath) — Maps 2D pixel coordinates from the camera frame to physical servo pan/tilt angles using a RANSAC-computed homography matrix.
Hardware Controller (TacticalSerial) — Thread-safe ESP32 serial communication at 115200 baud. Dispatches servo and laser commands in the format P{pan}T{tilt}L{laser}\n at up to 66Hz.

sections.heading: Application Tabs Overview
rows: Live feed, telemetry, start/stop engine, laser/alarm control, trim sliders
Model selection, confidence/IOU/FPS tuning, visualization settings
Full stereo calibration with chessboard image capture and RMS analysis
Click-and-steer laser alignment to generate homography matrix
Converts MATLAB .mat calibration files to Python .npz archives
Manual servo control, serial connection management, sweep tests
Camera indices, AI model, resolution, COM port, calibration file paths
Real-time INFO/WARN/ERROR event stream from all subsystems

sections.heading: Quick Start Workflow
steps: Install all software dependencies: CUDA 11.8+, cuDNN, TensorRT, CH340 driver
Connect hardware: Left camera (USB), Right camera (USB), ESP32 (USB-Serial)
Launch NEXUS.exe or run python main.py from the project root
Go to PY CALIBRATION → Capture 20+ stereo chessboard image pairs → Run Calibration (target RMS < 1.0 px)
Go to Laser Alignment → Click points on video, steer laser to match → Save 12+ points → Compute Matrix
Go to Dashboard → Click ▶ START ENGINE → System begins real-time tracking

sections.heading: Calibration Mode Indicators
sections.content: The Dashboard shows a real-time VISION MODE indicator that reflects the calibration state of the running engine:
rows: Both stereo and laser matrices loaded — full 3D depth + accurate targeting
Stereo calibration loaded but no laser matrix — depth works, aim may drift
No calibration files — system runs in raw pixel mode, no depth estimation
